title: Reframe as discrete-time survival with censoring, then read out next-lap hazard
summary: The target is naturally a time-to-next-pit event, and survival literature explicitly shows discrete-time person-period framing lets you use standard classifiers while handling censoring correctly.
rationale: The target is naturally a time-to-next-pit event, and survival literature explicitly shows discrete-time person-period framing lets you use standard classifiers while handling censoring correctly. This sharpens the earlier hazard idea into a concrete formulation: optimize the whole stop-timing process, then use hazard at t+1 as the submission probability.
implementation_hint: For each row, build remaining-laps-to-next-pit labels on train; if no later pit exists in that race, mark as right-censored at race end. Train either a discrete-time hazard model on horizon bins 1..H or an XGBoost AFT model on interval labels. Use auxiliary losses over 1-3 lap horizons, but submit only hazard(h=1).
expected_effect: Better ranking near pit windows and fewer inconsistent probabilities across adjacent laps from the same stint.
risk: Data expansion can become heavy, and survival formulations may overfit if horizons are too granular for rare late-race events.
